sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.128 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$T$be a finite$p$-group admitting an elementary abelian group of automorphisms$A$of order$p^{2}$such that in the semidirect product$P=TA$every element of$P T$has order$p$. Does it follow that$T$is of exponent~$p$? { E.\,Jabara} % jabara@unive.it {18th Issue (2014)} ~ { Problems from the 18th Issue (2014)} %

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
